incorrects.description: Student placed wrong product tile on first attempt

steps.text: Not quite. Count the sheep in one cluster, then skip count by that number for all 5 clusters.

incorrects.description: Student placed wrong product tile on second attempt

steps.text: Here's what helps: one cluster has 7 sheep. Now skip count by 7s for the 5 clusters: 7, 14, 21, 28, 35. What's the product?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: Clusters highlight one at a time as guide skip counts: 7, 14, 21, 28, 35.

steps.text: Let me show you. One cluster has 7 sheep. I skip count by 7s: first cluster, 7. Second cluster, 14. Third, 21. Fourth, 28. Fifth, 35. Five groups of 7 equals 35. The product is 35 because 5 times 7 has the same value as 35.
